source.derivation: Directly derived from the internship resume layout.
source.contentPolicy: Use this file as layout and writing-style reference only. Do not copy identity, links, project claims, company names, dates, or education unless separately supplied as candidate data.

summary.heading: SUMMARY
summary.writingStyle: student or early-career positioning; emphasize projects, coursework, practical tools, AI or software workflows, and ability to learn quickly

education.heading: EDUCATION

coursework.style: comma-separated list spanning one or two lines

education.writingStyle: make education visible because internship resumes rely on school context

bulletWriting.pattern: Action verb + technical task + tool or method + result or purpose

technicalProjects.heading: TECHNICAL PROJECTS
technicalProjects.bulletCount: 4-6 per major project, 3-4 per smaller project
technicalProjects.writingStyle: projects should prove the JD skills with concrete implementation details, repositories, tools, and debugging work

keySkills.heading: KEY SKILLS
keySkills.writingStyle: compact, ATS-readable, strongest JD matches first within each category

certifications.heading: CERTIFICATIONS
certifications.writingStyle: include only relevant certifications or training links

links.heading: LINKS
links.writingStyle: place extra links here rather than overloading the header

aiInstructions.layoutPriority: Follow this section order and header structure exactly.
aiInstructions.contentGeneration: Generate internship-level content that sounds capable but not senior.
aiInstructions.jdTailoring: Move JD-matching projects and skills higher. Keep education and projects prominent.
aiInstructions.outputRequirement: Return structured resume JSON matching this blueprint; do not return markdown.
